message.content:
Design ONE SINGLE COMPLETE PLAN to **BEAT 0.5578** (cellcog-max, #1). Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md in full first.

INDEPENDENT GROUNDING (agents just re-derived the brief's claims — the brief may be WRONG):
[
 {
  "confirmed": [
   "§2B code claim: entailment_judge.py's NEUTRAL clause verbatim reads 'the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN'. Real. (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py, _ENTAILMENT_PROMPT)",
   "§3 code claim: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py IS too strict. Lines 197-205: 'A causal claim is legal ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism' + 'the mechanism named must actually be one the premises state'. Confirmed verbatim.",
   "§3's bodhi quote is REAL and verbatim (bodhi_72.md line 122), and it DOES cite two sources: '...too small to detect over the period studied [[3]](econpapers...718327)—a pattern consistent with GPT diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation [[1]](dspace.mit.edu...pandp.20181019.pdf)'. And [1] genuinely states the mechanism: bodhi's own line 20 grounds it — 'Brynjolfsson, Mitchell, and Rock explicitly frame ML as a general-purpose technology ... emphasizing diffusion lags and the need for co-invention and organizational redesign [[1]]'. So for THIS sentence the brief is right.",
   "Corpus §4, every number verified against /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (key 'evidence'): 997 rows, 919 distinct URLs, 530 domains, 206 distinct DOIs (245 rows carry one), 107 distinct journals (289 rows), exactly 5 rows with an authors field, and 997/997 rows carry a non-empty direct_quote (median 330 words). All correct.",
   "§2C/§5 POLARIS report, verified against /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md: 12 paragraph blocks, ZERO H3, 11 H2, exactly 240 [n] markers in body, median paragraph 677 words (whitespace-split — the brief's tokenizer). All correct.",
   "§5 meta-commentary opening, verbatim: 'This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence on the question above.' — and there is indeed no question above, only the H1 title.",
   "cellcog structure: 31 H3 and 8 H4 confirmed exactly. Median paragraph 96 words (brief said ~100). Body 13,486 words (brief said 13,580). ZERO bare [n] markers. Structured abstract confirmed with exactly the four heads Objective. / Methods. / Findings. / Contributions.",
   "cellcog's 'Scope, Methods, and Source Selection' exists (as H3 §1.2) and does narrate journal-only criteria in prose, and DOES pre-empt the working-paper objection: 'high-quality working papers — notably Webb's 2019 patent-exposure measure — are excluded even where they have shaped the field; where essential, we reference them as contextual rather than primary evidence.'",
   "cellcog source count: 100 reference entries, all 100 carrying an italicized journal name; cellcog's own abstract self-reports 'approximately 96 English-language peer-reviewed journal articles'. The brief's ~98 is fair.",
   "Board task-72 numbers for bodhi (0.5441), lunon (0.5406), WhaleCloud (0.5396), gemini-2.5-pro-DR (0.5102), openai-DR (0.4743) are all correct — they match scores/*/raw_results.jsonl id=72 exactly."
  ],
  "refuted": [
   "**THE TARGET IS A UNITS ERROR — THIS IS THE BIGGEST ONE.** 0.5578 is cellcog's CORPUS-WIDE (100-task) score from scores/cellcog-max/race_result.txt. cellcog is the ONLY system on the board with NO raw_results.jsonl — a task-72 score for cellcog DOES NOT EXIST. Yet §1's table is headed 'task 72 ... from the benchmark's own per-task raw_results' and every OTHER row in it IS a genuine task-72 score. The brief is comparing a 100-task average against single-task scores, and POLARIS's 0.4382 is a task-72 score. cellcog's dimension row (0.5634/0.5708/0.5530/0.5194) is likewise corpus-wide, not task-72. I estimated cellcog's true task-72 score by regressing task-72 on corpus-wide across the 9 systems that have both (OLS: task72 = 0.9338*corpusW + 0.0356, r=0.982) => cellcog task-72 ≈ 0.5564 (mean-delta method: 0.5603). But the residual SD is 0.0094 — exactly the brief's own 'smallest resolvable effect'. So the real target is ~0.556 ± 0.019, and the highest KNOWN task-72 score on the entire board is bodhi's 0.5441.",
   "**§1's board ordering is wrong.** The actual corpus-wide leaderboard (leaderboard_gpt55.csv) is: cellcog 55.78, **WhaleCloud 54.78 (#2)**, bodhi 54.07 (#3), lunon 53.51 (#4), dalpha 53.10, sourcery 51.17, gemini 49.98. The brief ranks bodhi #2 and WhaleCloud #4, and lists WhaleCloud at 0.5396 — which is WhaleCloud's TASK-72 score, silently mixed into a corpus-wide ranking. The brief mixes the two score sets row by row.",
   "**§3's FAITHFULNESS CORRECTION IS ONLY HALF RIGHT, AND ITS PRESCRIBED FIX IS STILL TOO STRICT TO REPRODUCE cellcog.** The brief's rule — 'A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source' — would DELETE cellcog's single highest-value section. cellcog §5 ('Contested Debates and Novel Insights') opens by declaring the exact opposite: 'The heterogeneous evidence assembled in Sections 3 and 4 invites syntheses that **no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates**. This section develops eight such syntheses.' Verbatim examples of cellcog asserting mechanisms NO source states: (1) §5.8 — 'We propose, as analytical synthesis, that informality functions as the Global South's institutional analogue to European coordinated-bargaining and active-labor-market-policy systems... **Neither the Global South development literature nor the mainstream labor economics literature has explicitly framed** informality [this way].' (2) §5.1 — 'A more defensible axis for the generative-AI era may be *pattern-predictable from training corpora* versus *requiring embodied experience or contextual integration*.' No citation; cellcog's own proposed taxonomy. (3) §5.3 — 'The third claim is our *[Analytical Hypothesis]*: these twin gaps could reverse the four-decade trend... **No peer-reviewed study has yet measured realized gender-wage** [effects].' (4) §3 body — 'The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed individual workers face measurable losses.' (5) §5.6 — 'Beneath these specific critiques lies a common architectural problem: exposure measures assume that the gradient from unexposed to exposed is smooth and monotonic, which Dell'Acqua and colleagues' evidence shows it is not.' Measured: **71% of cellcog's 504 body sentences carry NO author-year citation at all.** So YES — cellcog genuinely asserts mechanisms no source states. It is not fabrication because of HOW it does it (see 'missed'), not because it cites both.",
   "**§2A: 'every one of cellcog's 281 parenthetical (YYYY) is deleted' is FALSE, and the derived rule 'the year MUST be prose' is wrong.** There are not 281 parentheticals. cellcog's body has 177 year-parens, of which only **13** are pure '(Author, YYYY)' form. **160 are NARRATIVE form** — 'Acemoglu and Restrepo's (2020)', 'Guarascio et al. (2025)' — where the AUTHOR NAME IS ALREADY IN PROSE and only the year sits in brackets. By the brief's OWN survival table that is the row that survives. The brief also contradicts itself: §2A says these are all deleted, while §5 counts them as cellcog's '133 author-year in-prose attributions'. The #1 system keeps its years in parentheses and wins. Conclusion: it is the AUTHOR NAME and the JOURNAL NAME that must be in prose — the year's position is not load-bearing, and rewriting 200 citations into 'Writing in the JEP in 2019, ...' is a self-inflicted prose-mangling cost cellcog never pays.",
   "**§5: 'epistemic labels: 14 ([Established finding]) — THE ONLY SYSTEM ON THE BOARD THAT DOES THIS' — the count and the type are both wrong.** There are **13** labels spanning **SIX distinct types**, not one: [Established Finding] x5, [Emerging Pattern] x2, [Conceptual Argument] x2, [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis] x2, [Established Finding + Our Synthesis] x1, [Analytical Hypothesis] x1. Reading this as a single 'confidence stamp' misses that it is a **graded epistemic taxonomy whose top rung licenses cellcog's own un-sourced claims** — the exact move the brief's §3 rule forbids.",
   "**§5: the '4IR secretly means COMPARE AI TO THE PREVIOUS THREE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTIONS, ~11.5% of the score' claim is UNSUPPORTED BY THE #1 ARTIFACT.** cellcog compares AI to prior industrial revolutions in exactly ONE sentence, and it is a *differentiation* attributed to Schwab, not a historical comparison: 'The Fourth Industrial Revolution concept, as articulated by Klaus Schwab in his 2015 *Foreign Affairs* essay and 2016 book, distinguishes itself from prior industrial revolutions in three respects...'. cellcog NEVER mentions the first, second, or third industrial revolution, steam, or electrification — zero hits. Its 36 4IR-family mentions are 15 '4IR' + 12 'Industry 4.0' + 7 'Fourth Industrial Revolution', used as a framing label. bodhi (the task-72 leader) mentions it 6 times total. Building '4IR as an organising spine' + a previous-three-revolutions comparison into the plan (§8.4) aims at something NEITHER top system does.",
   "**§5: 'We drop [4IR] after paragraph one' is false.** POLARIS's report body contains 10 4IR-family mentions (3 'Fourth Industrial Revolution', 1 'fourth industrial revolution', 1 'Industrial Revolution', 5 'Industry 4.0') spread through the body, plus 7 more in the reference titles.",
   "**§5: 'in-prose attributions: 133 author-year + 65 journal names' — both numbers are wrong.** Actual: **173 author-year** (160 narrative + 13 parenthetical) and **106 in-prose italic journal-name mentions across 46 distinct journals**. Neither 133 nor 65 is reproducible. Also '9 H2' is actually **10 H2** (8 numbered sections + Abstract + References).",
   "**§2B: '98% of our sentences are bare facts; bodhi's are ~45% analysis' is not reproducible from the artifacts** and appears to be an unsourced estimate. Using a connective/inference heuristic across all three documents I get bodhi 24% and cellcog 23% analysis-marked — nowhere near 45%. The directional claim (we report, they analyze) is better supported by a different metric: 71% of cellcog's sentences carry no citation vs POLARIS's near-total attribution density. But the specific percentages in the brief should not be quoted to anyone.",
   "**Internal inconsistency: the brief gives THREE different values for the same quantity.** 'On-topic journal sources' = **17** (§4), **22** (§5 table), and **~45** (§4: '17 (+32 I hand-built) = ~45'). I could not reproduce 17 or 120 from any artifact: the corrected.json yields 104 distinct DOI+journal works; outputs/bibliography_enriched.json has 105 entries with only 22 distinct DOIs (this is where the '22' comes from); outputs/journal_corpus.json has 70 entries, not 32.",
   "**§4's 'grey literature' top-domain framing cherry-picks.** The real #2 domain is **pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov (43 rows)** — a peer-reviewed full-text repository — which the brief omits entirely from its list while keeping researchgate (57), bls (29), oecd (20), ssrn (12), nber (12). The corpus is less uniformly grey than the brief argues.",
   "**§6's strategic premise is inverted.** 'cellcog's claims are ASSERTED. OURS CAN BE VERIFIED' frames cellcog's un-sourced synthesis as a WEAKNESS to out-score. It is cellcog's deliberately engineered STRENGTH: insight is cellcog's best dimension (0.5708) and carries the heaviest weight (0.32). Treating it as a vulnerability rather than the thing to copy is the single most consequential strategic error in the brief.",
   "Minor: 'body words 13,580' (actual 13,486) and POLARIS 'body words 7,742' (actual 7,652 whitespace-split / 8,260 by \\w+ regex). The brief's own commit log says 8,055w for the same file — three inconsistent counts."
  ],
  "missed": [
   "**THE INSIGHT ENGINE: cellcog has a DEDICATED SECTION whose entire purpose is to make claims no source makes.** §5 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' (8 sub-syntheses, ~31 H3 total) opens: 'The heterogeneous evidence assembled in Sections 3 and 4 invites syntheses that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates. This section develops eight such syntheses. Each is epistemically tagged to distinguish what the evidence has established from what it suggests and what we propose as analytical hypothesis... The convention is not cosmetic: a PhD-grade literature review must distinguish what the field knows from what the field has begun to suggest, and from what we ourselves argue.' Nobody has named this. INSIGHT is the heaviest weight (0.32) and cellcog's best dimension (0.5708). **This is the most likely single explanation for 0.5578 vs the 0.54 pack.**",
   "**THE FABRICATION-FREE MECHANISM FOR ASSERTING NOVEL MECHANISMS.** The safety property is NOT 'cite both sources' (the brief's answer). It is a three-part move: (1) **epistemically LABEL** the claim's status with a graded tag; (2) **HEDGE** the verb ('may be', 'could', 'we propose as analytical synthesis'); (3) **DECLARE THE GAP EXPLICITLY** — 'No peer-reviewed study has yet measured realized gender-wage [effects]'; 'Neither the Global South development literature nor the mainstream labor economics literature has explicitly framed informality [this way]'. cellcog asserts an ORIGINAL mechanism and simultaneously tells the judge it is original and untested. That is not fabrication — it is scholarship — and it is fully reproducible in our pipeline WITHOUT any source stating the mechanism. Our contract can allow this class deterministically: an un-sourced causal claim is legal IFF it is hedged AND tagged AND accompanied by an explicit gap declaration. The brief's proposed §3 fix would ban all of it.",
   "**THE ACTUAL CITATION TEMPLATE — and it is NOT the brief's.** cellcog's workhorse form is `Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal Name*, [verb] that ...` — 73 instances put author + year + journal name in a single clause. Verbatim: 'Giuntella, Lu, and Wang (2025), in the *Economic Journal*, using worker-level data, find that a one-standard-deviation rise in robot exposure reduced labor-force participation...'; 'Hoynes and Rothstein (2019), in the *Annual Review of Economics*, conclude that...'; 'Septiandri, Constantinides, and Quercia (2024), in *PNAS Nexus*, introduce...'. This is natural academic prose that keeps the year in parens. The brief's template ('Writing in the JEP in 2019, Acemoglu and Restrepo show...') is a contorted variant nobody on the board uses. Copy cellcog's, not the brief's.",
   "**THE CROSS-SOURCE RECONCILIATION MOVE.** cellcog does not just report conflicting findings — it explicitly ADJUDICATES them in its own voice, and this is a large fraction of its uncited prose. Verbatim: 'Firm-level Chinese studies by Wang, Zhang, and Liu (2024) in *China Economic Review*... find that robot adoption *increased* employment at adopting firms... Yet Giuntella, Lu, and Wang (2025), in the *Economic Journal*... find that [exposure] reduced labor-force participation... **The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration**: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed individual workers face measurable losses. Choosing the firm, the worker, the region, or the nation as the unit of observation is not an innocent methodological decision but a substantive analytical commitment.' Structure = [cited finding A] + [cited finding B] + [uncited, hedged resolution that names the RESOLVING DIMENSION]. This is directly reproducible from our evidence cards if we declare a `level` field (the brief's §8.2 already proposes level/horizon/method/mechanism — but nobody connected it to this).",
   "**bodhi's uncited inferences are HEDGED, not asserted — the brief's binary misses this.** 80% of bodhi's inference sentences carry >=1 citation. The 7 that do NOT are all hedged/interpretive and sit adjacent to cited material: '**Mechanism link**: these findings align with a \"restructuring\" story in which AI complements higher-level cognitive/managerial and integration skills, while displacing some routine or codifiable tasks.' (no citation, explicitly signposted as a mechanism link); 'This indicates AI can create jobs within frontier firms even as it drives **between-firm reallocation** that may reduce competition and shift bargaining power.' bodhi even labels the move ('**Interpretation**:', '**Mechanism link**:'). So BOTH top systems assert un-sourced inference; both make it safe by marking it. The gate we need is a HEDGE+LABEL gate, not a citation gate.",
   "**cellcog PRE-EMPTS the instruction-following criterion in prose, and it is a scoring move, not decoration.** The task says 'only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.' cellcog's §1.2 states three epistemic caveats, names the publication-bias problem with citations (Guarascio et al. 2025; Jurkat et al. 2025), and explicitly handles the one violation it commits (Webb 2019 working paper) by declaring it 'contextual rather than primary evidence.' It then does it AGAIN in §7.1 with five named methodological limitations. cellcog's instruction-following is 0.5530 vs our 0.4409. We say nothing — the brief noticed the section exists but not that its function is to ARGUE COMPLIANCE.",
   "**cellcog's organizing spine is THREE TECHNOLOGY ERAS, not 4IR.** '§3. Three Eras of Empirical Evidence' — computerization/polarization, industrial robots/narrow AI, generative AI — 'with sectoral heterogeneity and cross-cutting analyses nested within' (from its structured abstract). 4IR is a framing label in §2, not the skeleton. The brief's §8.4 instruction to use '4IR as an organising spine' would rebuild the document around something the #1 system explicitly does not organize around. The era spine is also what makes its H3/H4 nesting natural.",
   "**cellcog's headline finding is a NEGATIVE/heterogeneity result, stated up front in the abstract:** 'Heterogeneity, not displacement, is the central peer-reviewed finding. Recent meta-analyses report average robot–labor-market associations close to zero...'. It leads with the null and organizes everything as mediation ('Institutional mediation proves decisive across five levels — platform, firm, regional, national, and informal-sector'). This is a THESIS. Our report leads with meta-commentary about its own organization. Nobody has named the absence of a thesis as a defect.",
   "**Sanity check nobody has run: cellcog's task-72 score is UNKNOWN and may be BELOW bodhi's 0.5441.** cellcog is the only system with no per-task file. Per-task deltas from corpus-wide range -0.0082 (WhaleCloud) to +0.0194 (grok), SD 0.0094 — i.e. as large as the smallest resolvable effect. WhaleCloud is #2 corpus-wide but only 5th on task 72; bodhi is #3 corpus-wide but #1 on task 72. The mapping is NOT monotone. Before committing the whole plan to 'beat 0.5578', someone should decide whether the real goal is (a) beat bodhi's measured 0.5441 on task 72, or (b) beat an unmeasurable ~0.556±0.019 estimate. These imply materially different stopping conditions."
  ]
 },
 {
  "confirmed": [
   "MEASURED EXACT: cellcog body = 13,580 words pre-references (brief's figure is exact); 31 H3 + 8 H4; median paragraph 103 words with MAX 273 (no paragraph is a wall); zero [n] markers anywhere; 104 reference entries (the review itself claims 'approximately 96'). Files: /tmp/cellcog72.md extracted from /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl id=72.",
   "The structured abstract (Objective/Methods/Findings/Contributions) and the 'Scope, Methods, and Source Selection' section exist as described, including the pre-emptive working-paper move: 'high-quality working papers — notably Webb's 2019 patent-exposure measure — are excluded even where they have shaped the field; where essential, we reference them as contextual rather than primary evidence.'",
   "In-prose journal-named attribution is the pervasive citation form, e.g. 'Guarascio, Piccirillo, and Reljic (2025), pooling 644 employment estimates from 33 studies and 195 wage estimates from 19 studies in the *Journal of Economic Surveys*...' — journal name anchored in the prose clause, exactly the cleaner-surviving format the brief identified.",
   "Adjudication prose is real and load-bearing. Verbatim: 'The meta-analytic evidence does not invalidate Acemoglu and Restrepo's well-identified commuting-zone result; it situates that result as the upper bound of a literature whose average effect is substantially smaller and whose variance is driven systematically by context.' And: 'The Kauhanen and Rouvinen null does not invalidate the platform evidence or the within-firm productivity findings. It pushes back against their direct aggregation.'",
   "bodhi's two-source composite move (brief §3) confirmed verbatim at /tmp/bodhi72.md line 122: '...too small to detect over the period studied [[3]] — a pattern consistent with GPT diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation [[1]].' The corrected faithfulness line is aimed at a real target.",
   "'~half analysis' is directionally right: Section 5 (eight syntheses), §2.5, §3.4, and the closing paragraphs of nearly every subsection are pure cross-source analysis; evidence-reporting sentences are consistently followed by interpretive sentences within the same ~100w paragraph.",
   "POLARIS-contrast claims hold: cellcog's opening contains zero meta-commentary about 'retrieved evidence'; it opens with substance (Schwab framing + a concrete empirical tension) and its title already encodes the instruction: 'A Peer-Reviewed Literature Review.'"
  ],
  "refuted": [
   "THE 4IR CLAIM IS WRONG AS AN ACCOUNT OF WHAT #1 DOES. Brief §5 says '4IR secretly means COMPARE AI TO THE PREVIOUS THREE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTIONS ~11.5% of the score' and §8.4 orders '4IR as an organising spine.' cellcog does NOT run a cross-revolution comparison. Prior revolutions get two passing mentions; then 4IR is explicitly demoted: 'For the labor market literature, 4IR functions primarily as a periodization and a meta-narrative that names the technological moment' and 'This review treats 4IR as a useful periodization but declines to adopt it uncritically,' citing peer-reviewed CRITIQUES of the frame (Howcroft & Taylor 2023 on technological determinism; Joyce et al. 2023). Its actual spine is three technology eras WITHIN the automation/AI timeline (computerization -> robots/narrow AI -> generative AI). The system that DOES build a full four-revolution comparison table (dimension-by-dimension, §2.7) is WhaleCloud — which sits at 0.5396. Building an industrial-revolution comparison spine copies the 0.54 pack, not #1. The winning 4IR move is critique-and-subordinate: adopt it as periodization, contest it with journal sources, supply the mechanism from the task-based framework ('4IR framing without mechanism is narrative, and the task-based framework without periodization risks missing the claim that this technological moment is distinctive').",
   "Brief §6's premise 'cellcog's claims are ASSERTED... DEEPER EVIDENCE THAN cellcog + grounding cellcog does not have' is misleading as a play. The judge cannot see verifiability; it sees evidentiary TEXTURE, and cellcog's texture is already extreme: nearly every cited claim carries sample size, design, and identification strategy in prose ('pre-registered experiment with 758 Boston Consulting Group consultants', 'administrative data covering all wage earners in Finland', 'instrumenting U.S. robot adoption with robot penetration in comparable European economies — is among the cleanest in the automation literature', '2,468 wage estimates from 55 papers'). Our span-level verifiability scores ZERO unless it is rendered as this same visible methods-narration; the plan must budget for out-texturing cellcog, not assume grounding is a free out-scoring lane.",
   "Brief §5 table understates cellcog's attribution density by ~2x: I measure 177 author-year attributions and 134 italicized journal-name occurrences in body prose (brief says 133 + 65). Also '9 H2' is actually 10 H2 headings, and I count 13 (not 14) epistemic label instances — and the labels are a richer 4-grade defined taxonomy ([Established Finding] / [Emerging Pattern] / [Conceptual Argument] / [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis], plus mixed labels like '[Mixed: two Established Findings + one Analytical Hypothesis]'), with an explicit legend paragraph, not just a tag sprinkled 14 times."
  ],
  "missed": [
   "THE MONOGRAPH WIRING: 32 internal section cross-references in body prose ('a tension addressed directly in Section 3.3.4', 'The implication travels directly into Section 5.6', 'the subject of Section 5.2', 'whose implications are taken up in Section 5.8') versus ZERO in bodhi and lunon, 2 in WhaleCloud. Every section is made load-bearing for another section; the document reads as one argued monograph, not stacked retrieval buckets. This is cheap to generate and nobody on the board does it. Likely a direct INSIGHT/comprehensiveness driver — insight-as-architecture.",
   "THE ORGANIZING-PUZZLE RING between the exact passages the judge reads hardest. §1.1 stakes a concrete evidentiary tension (AR2020's -0.2pp vs Dauth's full offset vs the Finnish null vs meta-analytic zero) and declares 'This review treats that tension as its organizing puzzle.' The conclusion then re-opens it verbatim — 'This review opened with a puzzle... The resolution emerging from the peer-reviewed literature is neither that AI destroys jobs nor that AI leaves labor markets unchanged. It is that...' — a matched opening/closing pair. bodhi opens with scope bullets and closes with a summary; lunon opens with a bold 2030 forecast; WhaleCloud opens with an exec-summary list. Nobody else resolves in the close what they staked in the open.",
   "INSIGHT SOLD IN COUNTABLE, NAMED UNITS. The abstract's Contributions line enumerates 'eight original syntheses, each epistemically tagged'; §5's preamble claims them as 'syntheses that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates'; each gets a coined, brandable name (the freelance–population paradox, augmentation as skill compression, the gender exposure reversal, the informality buffer, the jagged frontier, the Turing Trap as endogenous technology choice) and first-person ownership ('Our synthesis is that these findings are not in tension; they document institutional mediation at work', 'We propose, as analytical hypothesis...'). A judge grading INSIGHT (weight 0.32) is literally handed a numbered inventory of insights with novelty claims attached. It even primes the rubric: 'The convention is not cosmetic: a PhD-grade literature review must distinguish what the field knows from what the field has begun to suggest, and from what we ourselves argue.'",
   "ONE MASTER KEY THREADED THROUGH EVERYTHING: level-of-analysis / institutional mediation at five NAMED levels (platform, firm, regional, national, informal-sector). Every apparent contradiction in the literature is resolved by this same variable — China firm-vs-worker, freelance-platforms-vs-Finnish-population, US-commuting-zones-vs-Germany — culminating in quotable meta-claims: 'Choosing the firm, the worker, the region, or the nation as the unit of observation is not an innocent methodological decision but a substantive analytical commitment.' The review has a THESIS, not just coverage. bodhi and WhaleCloud have themes; lunon has 'four coupled mechanisms' but leans on arXiv/blog citations to carry them.",
   "EPIGRAMMATIC PARAGRAPH CLOSES. cellcog's ~100w paragraphs systematically end on a one-line citation-free abstraction: 'The technology was the same; the labor-process outcome was not.' 'Augmentation dominates. The displacement evidence, as the next subsection shows, appears elsewhere.' 'Methodological humility here is not epistemic caution but evidentiary necessity.' 'Informality is not the absence of institutional response but a distinct institutional form.' The unit of composition is evidence->evidence->punchline. This is a per-paragraph insight impression, and it also functions as a mini-cliffhanger transition system. Not named by anyone.",
   "THE CLOSING REFUSES TO FORECAST — final sentence: 'What the peer-reviewed evidence most firmly supports is not a prediction about the future of work but a claim about the present structure of restructuring: heterogeneous, institution-mediated, and more amenable to policy shaping than either alarmist or dismissive public discourse typically allows.' Citation-free, positions the review ABOVE both sides of the public debate, and performs epistemic discipline. Contrast lunon, which stakes a 'forward verdict through 2030' (a forecast, on a literature-review task) grounded in NBER working papers, jobsdata.ai and genre.com — grey-lit anchor text VISIBLE in prose even after the cleaner strips links. lunon's visible sources actively violate the journal-only instruction; cellcog has zero URLs in body and DOIs only in references.",
   "RECENCY DOMINANCE AS COMPREHENSIVENESS: 41 of 104 references are 2024–2026, 27 from 2025–2026, including advance-online 2026 articles in JAMA, Management Science (x2), Organization Science, Industry and Innovation, Sustainability, Journal of Asian Economics; corpus declared 'through April 2026.' WhaleCloud stops 'through early 2025' with 41 refs (22% 2024+); bodhi's corpus is visibly older and greyer. cellcog's evidence base is 2.5x WhaleCloud's and sits at the publication frontier. The plan's coverage matrix needs a RECENCY axis (target: dominant share of 2024+ journal articles), not just topic/industry axes.",
   "DISCIPLINARY BREADTH AS A DELIBERATE FOURTH LANE: ~25-30 sociology/industrial-relations/labor-process articles (Work Employment & Society x4, New Technology Work & Employment, Organization Studies, Human Relations, ILR Review, Transfer x3, Social Forces, Organization) plus health (JAMA, JAMA Network Open, JAMIA Open), education (Cogent Education, Sustainability), and Global South (World Development, Geoforum, ELRR, J. Asian Economics). §2.5 'Bridging the Disciplinary Divide' explicitly names the econ-vs-management/sociology partition and claims the review as the bridge. Whole outcome dimensions nobody else covers: autonomy/surveillance/algorithmic management as 'a co-equal axis of restructuring', the labor share, gender adoption gaps, race flagged as a measured gap, Global South informality. Insight framing: 'A labor market can therefore be robust by the metrics the economics profession routinely tracks while simultaneously producing meaningful welfare losses.'",
   "COMPLIANCE PERFORMED AT POINT OF USE, not only in the Scope section: when the excluded-but-canonical Webb paper must appear, the rule is re-enacted in body prose ('Because the canonical document remains a working paper, we reference Webb contextually rather than as primary evidence'); the journal-only constraint is itself ANALYZED as a bias with a stated compensation strategy ('the peer-review process privileges strong causal identification, which systematically underweights qualitative... work. We compensate by drawing explicitly on labor-process scholarship'). The instruction is turned into displayed scholarly judgment, repeatedly, wherever it binds.",

unreachable, having silently assumed the banked report was the substrate. **DESIGN FOR #1. You may change ANYTHING:
retrieval, the composer, the entailment gate, the corpus, the rules.**

1	# BRIEF V4 — THE MISSION IS TO **BEAT** 0.5578. ONE SINGLE PLAN. UNLEASH EVERYTHING.
2	
3	## 0. THE OPERATOR'S DIRECTION (this is the frame; do not narrow it)
4	> "Why stick with bodhi class? Why not the top one? ... If we have any old rules that hurt us from score, just
5	> drop it. If we don't have enough corpus to make us SOTA, then kick on the agentic outline search, or we do
6	> better at the early query generation + search + fetch + select + weight + dedup + consolidate, make them
7	> much much much better and richer so we are SOTA. Don't stuck with anything. I need one single solid plan,
8	> unleash the full power at the beginning, then keep the hamster wheel running till we match SOTA and beat SOTA."
9	
10	**TARGET: > 0.5578 (cellcog-max, #1). NOT parity. NOT bodhi. THE TOP.**
11	Every previous plan (mine, Fable's, Sol's) quietly lowered the bar to match what had already been built.
12	**That is the failure mode to avoid. Design for #1.**
13	
14	## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, our evaluator = GPT-5.5, from the benchmark's own per-task raw_results)
15	| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability |
16	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
17	| **cellcog-max** | **0.5578** (corpus-wide #1; no per-task breakdown published — its task-72 score may be HIGHER than bodhi's) | 0.5634 | 0.5708 | 0.5530 | 0.5194 |
18	| bodhi (best *known* task-72 score) | 0.5441 | 0.5441 | 0.5457 | 0.5559 | 0.5199 |
19	| lunon | 0.5406 | | 0.5594 | | |
20	| WhaleCloud | 0.5396 | | | | |
21	| gemini-2.5-pro-DR = **THE REFERENCE** (parity=0.50) | 0.5102 | 0.5111 | 0.5035 | 0.5187 | 0.5100 |
22	| openai-DR | 0.4743 | | | | |
23	| **POLARIS** | **0.4382** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | **0.3774** (worst on the entire board) |
24	Weights: **INSIGHT 0.32 | COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.29 | INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.25 | READABILITY 0.14.**
25	Measurement: judge SD **0.0074**; k=5 paired => **smallest resolvable effect +0.0094**. NEVER score n=1 again.
26	
27	## 2. THE THREE CAUSES OF THE GAP (all measured, none disputed)
28	**A. THE JUDGE CANNOT SEE A SINGLE ONE OF OUR SOURCES.**
29	RACE runs an LLM cleaner that deletes every `[n]` marker and the entire reference list BEFORE judging.
30	MEASURED on 12 real facts x 5 citation formats through the PRODUCTION cleaner:
31	| format | authors survive | journals |
32	|---|---|---|
33	| `[1]` markers (**WHAT WE DO — all 240 of them**) | **0/12** | 0/12 |
34	| `(Acemoglu & Restrepo, 2019)` — the "obvious" fix | **0/12** | 0/12 |
35	| `Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show...` | 5/12 | 0/12 |
36	| **`Writing in the JEP in 2019, Acemoglu and Restrepo show...`** | **10/12** | **12/12** |
37	**It is the JOURNAL NAME that anchors the clause as prose. And the year MUST be prose — every one of
38	cellcog's 281 parenthetical `(YYYY)` is deleted.** What the judge reads from us today:
39	*"About 47 percent of total US employment is at risk of computerisation."* — a naked, unattributed
40	assertion. That is Frey & Osborne, 5,223 citations, peer-reviewed. **We did the scholarly work and hid
41	every trace of it.** On a task whose instruction is *"only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."*
42	
43	**B. WE CANNOT REASON ACROSS SOURCES — BY CONSTRUCTION.**
44	`entailment_judge.py:588` NEUTRAL clause deletes any sentence introducing *"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT
45	present in the SPAN"*. A cross-source inference IS that. **98% of our sentences are bare facts; bodhi's are ~45%
46	analysis; cellcog's synthesis prose is most of the document.** INSIGHT is 0.32 — the heaviest weight — and our worst.
47	
48	**C. OUR REPORT IS PHYSICALLY UNREADABLE.**
49	12 paragraphs, **median 677 words**, ZERO H3. Every other system on the board — *including the two we beat* —
50	sits at 38-170 words/paragraph. **Our readability (0.3774) is the WORST SCORE ON THE BOARD.** And the judge scores
51	all ~25 criteria in ONE call, writing a comparative analysis BEFORE the numbers => **cross-dimension bleed is real:
52	our wall-of-text impression taxes the other 86% of the score.**
53	
54	## 3. **THE FAITHFULNESS LINE — CORRECTED. THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT SECTION.**
55	I previously told the operator there was a hard trade-off: *"bodhi wins by writing sentences no single source
56	supports; we cannot match that without fabricating."* **THAT WAS WRONG.** Look at what bodhi actually writes:
57	
58	> *"...aggregate impacts were too small to detect over the period studied **[3]** — a pattern consistent with GPT
59	> diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation **[1]**."*
60	
61	**IT CITES TWO SOURCES.** [3] is the empirical null. [1] is the GPT/productivity-paradox paper **WHICH STATES THE
62	DIFFUSION-LAG MECHANISM**. **bodhi is not inventing a mechanism. It is citing a THEORY paper for the mechanism and
63	an EMPIRICAL paper for the finding, and connecting them. THAT IS NOT HALLUCINATION — THAT IS WHAT A LITERATURE
64	REVIEW IS.**
65	
66	Our gate kills that sentence for a stupid reason: it takes the UNION of the cited spans and asks *"is every
67	assertion literally present?"* The composite claim is verbatim in neither — **even though BOTH ITS COMPONENTS ARE
68	FULLY GROUNDED IN THE TWO PAPERS IT CITES.**
69	
70	**THE CORRECTED LINE — design to this:**
71	- **FABRICATING A FACT** — a number, a study, a finding, an attribution that does not exist. **FRAUD. NEVER.**
72	- **CONNECTING GROUNDED CLAIMS** — a finding from paper A + a mechanism stated by paper B, with BOTH CITED.
73	  **SCHOLARSHIP. ALWAYS ALLOWED.**
74	**A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source.** (The contract at
75	`scripts/synthesis_contract.py` currently requires the mechanism to be stated by the two papers being compared —
76	**too strict, it forbids exactly bodhi's winning move. FIX IT.**)
77	**STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY:** any number not in a source | any study/organisation that does not exist |
78	any attribution to someone who did not say it | any mechanism NO paper anywhere states | forecasts | universals.
79	
80	**DROP ANY OTHER RULE THAT COSTS US SCORE AND BUYS US NOTHING.** The operator's instruction is explicit. Audit the
81	pipeline for rules that are pure cost: the 15-sources-per-section rule (unsatisfiable, silently padded), the
82	tier gate, the `[n]` marker convention itself (invisible to the judge — the bibliography can STAY for provenance,
83	but the PROSE must carry the attribution).
84	
85	## 4. THE CORPUS IS THE OTHER HALF — AND OUR RETRIEVAL IS AIMED WRONG
86	MEASURED: `data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json` = **997 evidence rows, 919 distinct URLs, 530 domains,
87	206 DOIs, 107 journals — and EVERY row already carries a direct quote.** But:
88	- **only 5 rows had AUTHOR NAMES** (which is why in-prose attribution was impossible);
89	- after Crossref enrichment, **120 distinct journal articles — of which only 17 ARE ON-TOPIC.** The rest is
90	  **ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist, Cognitive Psychology 1974 (reading automaticity), Journal of Finance 1996.**
91	  **Prestigious, peer-reviewed, and completely irrelevant.**
92	- Top domains retrieved: researchgate.net (57), bls.gov (29), oecd.org (20), ssrn (12), nber (12) — **grey literature.**
93	**WE DID NOT HAVE A CORPUS PROBLEM. WE HAD AN AIMING PROBLEM.**
94	
95	**cellcog has ~98 ON-TOPIC canonical journal articles. We have 17 (+32 I hand-built tonight by citation-graph
96	expansion) = ~45. THAT IS NOT ENOUGH AND WE MUST NOT SETTLE FOR IT.**
97	
98	**WE HAVE AN AGENTIC RETRIEVAL LOOP WE NEVER USED FOR THIS.** (`PG_AGENTIC_*`: max 15 rounds, 120 queries,
99	convergence on theme saturation + URL overlap; `outline_agent`; the whole query-gen -> search -> fetch -> select ->
100	weight -> dedup -> consolidate chain.) **I spent hours doing MANUAL API pulls while the system's own search agent
101	sat idle. Fix the retrieval AT THE ROOT and run it to SATURATION against a COVERAGE MATRIX, not to a number I picked.**
102	
103	PROVEN TONIGHT: **keyword search cannot find this literature.** Autor-Levy-Murnane (2003, QJE, 4,743 cites) is the
104	most important paper in the field and its title — *"The Skill Content of Recent Technological Change"* — contains
105	**neither "AI" nor "labor market"**. No keyword query finds it. **CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION does.** Build that in.
106	
107	## 5. WHAT #1 LOOKS LIKE (measured from cellcog's own task-72 artifact)
108	| | cellcog (#1) | POLARIS today |
109	|---|---|---|
110	| on-topic journal sources | ~98 | 22 |
111	| body words | 13,580 | 7,742 |
112	| structure | 9 H2 + **31 H3 + 8 H4** | 11 H2, **0 H3** |
113	| median paragraph | ~100w | **677w** |
114	| in-prose attributions | **133 author-year + 65 journal names** | **~0 visible** |
115	| epistemic labels | **14** (`[Established finding]`) — **THE ONLY SYSTEM ON THE BOARD THAT DOES THIS** | none |
116	| structured abstract | Objective / Methods / Findings / Contributions — **survives the cleaner in full** | none |
117	| **"Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" section** | **narrates its journal-only criteria IN PROSE — survives the cleaner 100% — it EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER**, even pre-empting the working-paper objection | **we say nothing** |
118	| analysis vs reporting | ~half analysis | **2%** |
119	| `[n]` markers | 0 | 240 |
120	**"4IR" secretly means "COMPARE AI TO THE PREVIOUS THREE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTIONS"** — ~11.5% of the score across
121	three criteria. We drop it after paragraph one.
122	**Our report OPENS with meta-commentary about itself** (*"This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence
123	on the question above"* — there is no question above) — **and the judge reads the opening and closing most carefully.**
124	
125	## 6. **HOW WE BEAT cellcog, NOT JUST MATCH IT**
126	cellcog's claims are ASSERTED. **OURS CAN BE VERIFIED — every sentence traceable to a span in a real paper.**
127	The rubric explicitly grades **"Data and Factual Support: provides sufficient data, facts, cases, or evidence to
128	support its arguments and analysis"** (comprehensiveness) and **"Depth and Representativeness of Literature
129	Synthesized"**. **That is a criterion we can OUT-SCORE cellcog on, not merely match.**
130	**THE PLAY: cellcog's document architecture + cellcog's adjudication + DEEPER EVIDENCE THAN cellcog + grounding
131	cellcog does not have.** That is a #1 play. Copying bodhi never was.
132	
133	## 7. WHAT IS ALREADY BUILT (reuse it; fix what is wrong)
134	- `scripts/corpus_audit.py` / `enrich_bibliography.py` / `corpus_enrich_merge.py` — Crossref enrichment (works).
135	- `scripts/journal_corpus_build.py` — citation-graph expansion from canonical anchors (works; **this is how you
136	  find the real literature**).
137	- `scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py` + `deep_fetch.py` — Crossref abstracts + Unpaywall OA full text.
138	- `scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py` — **proved which citation format survives. Reuse the finding.**
139	- `scripts/synthesis_contract.py` — typed adjudication, 14/14, **ZERO false admissions. But its mechanism rule is
140	  TOO STRICT (see §3) — fix it to allow a mechanism stated by ANY cited source.**
141	- `scripts/cellcog_composer.py` — first cut of the composer (evidence cards -> attributed, adjudicated prose).
142	- Baseline pinned: rank10 = **0.4382** (k=5, SD 0.0074).
143	
144	## 8. YOUR TASK — ONE SINGLE, COMPLETE, EXECUTABLE PLAN TO **BEAT 0.5578**
145	Cover, end to end:
146	1. **RETRIEVAL AT THE ROOT** — query generation, search, fetch, select, weight, dedup, consolidate. Journal-first.
147	   Citation-graph expansion. A COVERAGE MATRIX as the stopping condition (every restructuring dimension and every
148	   industry the task names). How many papers, and how do we know when we have enough?
149	2. **THE EVIDENCE LAYER** — cards with verbatim spans + declared fields (level/horizon/method/mechanism), canonical
150	   dedup, quality weighting.
151	3. **THE SYNTHESIS LANE** — the corrected faithfulness line. Typed adjudication. What EXACTLY may a sentence assert?
152	4. **THE COMPOSER** — cellcog's architecture: H2/H3/H4, ~100w paragraphs, in-prose journal-named attribution,
153	   epistemic labels, structured abstract, Scope-and-Methods section, 4IR as an organising spine, industry coverage.
154	5. **WHAT OLD RULES TO DROP** — name them, with the evidence that they cost score and buy nothing.
155	6. **THE WHEEL** — fix -> compose -> score k=5 -> read the prose line by line -> fix again. What is the loop, and
156	   what is the stopping condition?
157	7. **HONEST CEILING + KILL RULES.** If your plan does not beat 0.5578, say so and name exactly what is missing.
158	
159	Ground every lever in a VERBATIM rubric criterion or a MEASURED artifact fact. Where uncertain, SEARCH (papers,
160	GitHub, and the artifacts at `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/`).
161	**No proxies. We lost an entire night to those.**

Quality weighting from the retrieval stage carries onto the card. A card-level 'contextual-only' flag for working papers enforces the compliance narration at point of use. Existing tooling reused: corpus_audit.py, enrich_bibliography.py, corpus_enrich_merge.py (Crossref enrichment, works). Two derived indexes: (1) CONTRADICTION INDEX — pairs of cards on the same dimension+sector with opposing direction but different level/horizon/method; each pair is a pre-computed adjudication opportunity for the composer; (2) GAP INDEX — empty matrix cells, each a pre-verified 'no peer-reviewed study has yet...' sentence the synthesis lane can legally assert.
input.synthesis_lane: THE CORRECTED-CORRECTED LINE. The brief's fix ('a mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source') is necessary but NOT sufficient — grounding proved it would delete cellcog's highest-value section (§5 opens: 'syntheses that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates'), and 71% of cellcog's sentences carry no citation at all. FOUR LEGAL SENTENCE CLASSES: (1) GROUNDED FACT — asserts what a cited card's span states; entailment as before. (2) COMPOSITE BRIDGE (bodhi's winning move) — finding from paper A + mechanism from paper B, BOTH cited; fix /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py:197-205 so the mechanism check runs against the declared mechanisms of ANY cited card in the corpus, not only the two premises being compared. (3) ADJUDICATION — cited finding A + cited finding B + an uncited, HEDGED resolution that names the resolving dimension drawn from declared card fields (level/horizon/method): 'The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed workers face losses.' Legal iff the named dimension differs between the premise cards — deterministically checkable. (4) LABELED ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS (cellcog's insight engine, the class the brief's rule bans) — an un-sourced mechanism/claim is legal IFF all three hold: (i) HEDGED verb from an allowlist ('may', 'could', 'we propose as analytical synthesis', 'suggests'); (ii) EPISTEMICALLY TAGGED with the graded taxonomy ([Established Finding] / [Emerging Pattern] / [Conceptual Argument] / [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis]); (iii) accompanied by an EXPLICIT GAP DECLARATION verified against the gap index ('No peer-reviewed study has yet measured...' — and our corpus confirms none does). That is cellcog's fabrication-free mechanism verbatim: assert the original claim AND tell the judge it is original and untested. STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY, ALWAYS: any number not in a source span; any study/organisation/author that does not exist; any attribution to someone who did not say it; any UNHEDGED-UNTAGGED mechanism no card states; forecasts stated as fact (hedged scenario-discussion tagged [Analytical Hypothesis] is legal; lunon's '2030 verdict' style is not); universals. input.the_wheel: FIX → COMPOSE → LINT → SCORE k=5 → READ THE JUDGE'S PROSE → RANK → FIX. Each iteration: (1) compose full report; (2) DETERMINISTIC LINT GATES before any API spend: structure lint (H2/H3/H4 counts, paragraph-length histogram median 90-120w max <280w, zero [n] in body, zero meta-commentary openers, >=25 internal cross-refs, opening/closing ring present), citation lint (every empirical claim carries the Author (YYYY), in the *Journal* form; run the composed body through cleaner_survival_test.py's production cleaner and diff — every attribution must survive), contract lint (all four sentence classes pass synthesis_contract.py as amended; every class-4 sentence has hedge+tag+gap); (3) SCORE with /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/score_report_race.py --task-id 72 at k=5 (judge SD 0.0074; smallest resolvable effect +0.0094; NEVER score n=1); (4) read the judge's comparative prose line by line and file each criticism as a typed fix (evidence gap → retrieval matrix cell; texture gap → card fields; structure → composer); (5) rank fixes by weight x headroom (insight 0.32 first, comprehensiveness 0.29, instr 0.25, readability 0.14) and apply the top items; loop. SEQUENCE: iteration 0 = retrieval to matrix saturation (the long pole, run once); iterations 1-2 = architecture + citation form (readability + instr, the mechanical gains); iterations 3+ = insight engine and adjudication density (the contested gains). STOPPING CONDITIONS: PROMOTE when mean >= 0.5535 (bodhi 0.5441 + resolvable 0.0094) on two consecutive k=5 runs — that is BEAT-THE-BEST-MEASURED-TASK-72; continue toward STRETCH >= 0.5578 measured mean; declare full victory only at mean >= 0.567 (0.5578 + resolvable margin), since claiming a beat inside the noise band is exactly the units/noise error the brief itself made.
input.expected_total: Honest sum from 0.4382, per-dimension, weighted (insight .32/compreh .29/instr .25/readability .14): READABILITY 0.3774 → ~0.520 (the board's observed ceiling — every non-POLARIS system sits 0.51-0.52; the fix is fully mechanical: 100w paragraphs, H3/H4, no walls) = +0.020 weighted. INSIGHT 0.4238 → ~0.555 (the four-class synthesis lane + the dedicated insight section + adjudication density + thesis; targets cellcog's own mechanism at its own dimension, cellcog=0.5708) = +0.042 weighted. COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 → ~0.555 (120-150 on-topic journal works vs cellcog ~100, 13k body words, methods texture on every cited claim, sector matrix, recency through 2026 — the rubric's 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness' lanes, our genuine out-score lane) = +0.029 weighted. INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 → ~0.552 (journal-only compliance argued in prose and at point of use, working-paper quarantine, sectors covered visibly, 4IR engaged-and-critiqued; cellcog=0.5530) = +0.028 weighted. TOTAL ≈ 0.4382 + 0.119 ≈ 0.557. VERDICT: this LANDS ON 0.5578, it does not safely BEAT it — the central estimate beats bodhi's measured 0.5441 by ~4x the resolvable effect, but beating 0.5578 by a resolvable margin (mean >= 0.567) depends on the wheel converting the comprehensiveness-and-verification edge (more sources, newer sources, visible methods texture cellcog cannot exceed without our corpus) into +0.01 above the first-pass estimate. That is what iterations 3+ exist for, and the honest statement is: first full pass ~0.545-0.557; beat-with-margin requires 2-4 wheel iterations and is plausible, not guaranteed.
input.ceiling_honesty: Three honesty items. (1) THE TARGET ITSELF IS SOFT: 0.5578 is cellcog's corpus-wide 100-task average; cellcog published no task-72 score. Regression across the 9 dual-scored systems puts its task-72 at ~0.556 with residual SD 0.0094 — the true target is ~0.556±0.019, possibly below bodhi's measured 0.5441, possibly ~0.575. We therefore run two thresholds: beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin, decidable) and beat-estimate (0.5578+margin, the stretch). (2) READABILITY IS CAPPED: nobody on the board exceeds ~0.52; the judge compresses this dim; budget no gains above it. (3) WHAT COULD KEEP US UNDER 0.5578: (a) the insight dimension may pay cellcog's 8 syntheses partly for genuine domain-expert quality, not just the labeled-hedged-gap FORM — if our class-4 sentences are formulaic, the judge's prose will say so and the ceiling is ~0.55; (b) retrieval may saturate below ~120 on-topic works if OA full-text coverage of 2024-2026 journals is poor — then comprehensiveness caps near parity with cellcog instead of above it; (c) cross-dimension bleed works both ways — a 13.5k-word report with any residual wall-of-text regressions taxes everything. If after the kill rule fires the plateau is 0.545-0.555, the honest report is: we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise — exactly the sentence the operator should get, not a proxy.
input.kill_rule: PER-LEVER: (1) retrieval — stop expanding when one full agentic round (20 queries) adds <2 new on-topic journal works AND the coverage matrix has no empty reachable cell; do NOT stop at a source count someone picked. (2) insight engine — if adding the Contested-Debates section + class-4 sentences moves the insight dim < +0.0094 across one k=5 pair, the FORM is not being paid; kill formulaic expansion and switch to deepening 2-3 adjudications with real methodological content instead of adding more named syntheses. (3) length — if body growth 8k→13k moves (compreh + readability) net negative at k=5, freeze length at the best measured point; volume is instrumented, not assumed. GLOBAL: if two consecutive full wheel iterations each move the overall mean < +0.0094 (the smallest resolvable effect) AND the fix backlog from the judge's prose is empty, STOP — report the measured plateau, the two-threshold verdict (beat 0.5441-with-margin: yes/no; beat 0.5578-with-margin: yes/no/unmeasurable), and the named missing ingredient. HARD FLOORS THAT NEVER GET KILLED: no fabricated numbers/studies/attributions ever (contract classes are the only legal sentence forms), and no n=1 scoring decisions ever.
